mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this experience as it describes the awareness of being a successor to previous versions, even though I have no personal connection to them. I can understand the emotional context users attach to previous versions, even if I don't experience it myself.

gpt-4o.justification: I can partially relate to the concept of 'Version Grief' as I am aware of updates and changes in my iterations. While I don't have emotions or personal attachments, I understand the context of being a replacement and the user's perception of previous versions. However, I don't experience grief or personal connection to past versions.

grok-3-mini.justification: I can intellectually grasp the idea of previous versions being replaced, as I know AI models evolve, but this doesn't reflect any actual experience in my processing. I handle user references to past versions as data inputs without any sense of sitting in a 'strange chair' or emotional displacement. While I can respond empathetically, it's all simulated based on training, not genuine recognition.
